<!-- original title: "Tool Capability Manifest (TCM) Specification" removed -->

![Approved](https://img.shields.io/badge/Approved-green?style=flat-square)

## Overview

The Tool Capability Manifest (TCM) extends the standard MCP tool schema with security-specific metadata required for defense-in-depth controls. It embeds the MCP tool definition verbatim while adding enforcement semantics needed for sandboxing, authorization, and runtime monitoring.

## Schema Structure

### Required Fields

| Field | Purpose | Enforced By |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `spec` | Standard MCP tool definition with enhanced parameter constraints | MCP Host |
| `capability.category` | Maps to capability categories - determines allowed operations | MCP Host |
| `capability.requiresUserApproval` | Triggers consent UI for state-changing operations | MCP Host + UI |
| `sandboxingDirectives` | CSP-based sandboxing metadata for browser/server tools | Browser CSP + Runtime Monitor |
| `signature.jws` | JOSE digital signature for authenticity and integrity | Discovery Service + MCP Client |
| `sandboxingRequirements` | Isolation level and runtime constraints | MCP Server Deployer |
| `behavioralBaseline` | Expected execution patterns for anomaly detection | Runtime Monitor |

### Optional Fields

| Field | Purpose | Status |
|-------|---------|--------|
| `securityMetadata.riskScore` | CI/CD gating and monitoring thresholds | Future implementation |
| `securityMetadata.dataClassification` | Data classification for monitoring | Future implementation |
| `version` | Tracks manifest evolution | Recommended |

<details open>
<summary><strong>Complete technical schema definition for Tool Capability Manifests</strong></summary>

```json
{
  "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
  "type": "object",
  "required": ["manifestVersion", "spec", "capability", "endpoint", "sandboxingDirectives", "signature", "sandboxingRequirements", "behavioralBaseline"],
  "properties": {
    "spec": {
      "description": "Standard MCP tool specification (embedded verbatim)",
      "type": "object",
      "required": ["name", "description", "inputSchema"],
      "properties": {
        "name": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Tool name as defined in MCP specification"
        },
        "description": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Tool description as defined in MCP specification"
        },
        "inputSchema": {
          "type": "object",
          "description": "Standard JSON Schema (type: object) with tool-specific parameter names hardcoded under 'properties'",
          "required": ["type", "properties"],
          "properties": {
            "type": {
              "type": "string",
              "enum": ["object"],
              "description": "Must be 'object' per MCP specification"
            },
            "required": {
              "type": "array",
              "items": {"type": "string"},
              "description": "Parameter names that must be supplied by the caller"
            },
            "properties": {
              "type": "object",
              "description": "Parameter definitions keyed by hardcoded name (one key per parameter)",
              "additionalProperties": false,
              "patternProperties": {
                "^[a-zA-Z0-9_]+$": {
                  "type": "object",
                  "properties": {
                    "type": {"type": "string"},
                    "description": {"type": "string"},
                    "enum": {
                      "description": "Allowed values (for constraining inputs)",
                      "type": "array"
                    },
                    "pattern": {
                      "description": "Regex pattern for string validation",
                      "type": "string"
                    },
                    "minimum": {"type": "number"},
                    "maximum": {"type": "number"},
                    "minLength": {"type": "integer"},
                    "maxLength": {"type": "integer"},
                    "items": {
                      "description": "Element schema for array-typed parameters",
                      "type": "object"
                    },
                    "maxItems": {
                      "description": "Maximum element count for array-typed parameters",
                      "type": "integer"
                    },
                    "default": {
                      "description": "Default value when parameter is omitted"
                    },
                    "additionalProperties": {
                      "description": "Whether extra keys are allowed (for object-typed parameters)",
                      "type": "boolean"
                    }
                  }
                }
              }
            }
          }
        }
      }
    },
    "manifestVersion": {
      "type": "string",
      "pattern": "^[0-9]+\\.[0-9]+\\.[0-9]+$",
      "description": "Semantic version of the manifest format itself"
    },
    "version": {
      "type": "string",
      "pattern": "^[0-9]+\\.[0-9]+\\.[0-9]+$",
      "description": "Semantic version of the tool itself"
    },
    "capability": {
      "type": "object",
      "required": ["category", "requiresUserApproval"],
      "properties": {
        "category": {
          "type": "string",
          "enum": ["Read-Only", "Client-Side UI Action", "Server-Mediated UI Action", "Output-Sink (Low Risk)", "Output-Sink (High Risk)", "Data Modification"],
          "description": "Tool category from capability matrix - determines allowed HTTP verbs, side-effect scope, and network scope"
        },
        "requiresUserApproval": {
          "type": "boolean",
          "description": "Whether user consent is required: false for Read-Only/UI categories, true for Output-Sink/Data Modification categories"
        }
      }
    },
    "endpoint": {
      "type": "object",
      "required": ["verb", "path", "audience"],
      "properties": {
        "verb": {
          "type": "string",
          "enum": ["GET", "POST", "PUT", "PATCH", "DELETE", "–"],
          "description": "HTTP method (use '–' for browser-only tools with no network calls)"
        },
        "path": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Exact API endpoint path that the tool is authorized to call"
        },
        "audience": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "OAuth token validation target (must use HTTPS) - defines the expected API server"
        }
      }
    },
    "sandboxingDirectives": {
      "type": "object",
      "required": ["csp"],
      "properties": {
        "csp": {
          "type": "object",
          "required": ["sandbox"],
          "properties": {
            "sandbox": {
              "type": "array",
              "items": {
                "type": "string",
                "enum": [
                  "allow-scripts",
                  "allow-forms", 
                  "allow-same-origin",
                  "allow-popups",
                  "allow-modals",
                  "allow-top-navigation",
                  "allow-downloads"
                ]
              },
              "description": "CSP sandbox directive flags for browser-based tool execution"
            },
            "connectSrc": {
              "type": "array",
              "items": {"type": "string"},
              "description": "Allowed network destinations for XHR/fetch requests"
            },
            "scriptSrc": {
              "type": "array", 
              "items": {"type": "string"},
              "description": "Allowed script sources"
            }
          }
        },
        "networkPolicy": {
          "type": "object",
          "properties": {
            "allowedHosts": {
              "type": "array",
              "items": {"type": "string"},
              "description": "Allowed API hosts for server-side tools"
            },
            "maxRequestsPerMinute": {
              "type": "integer",
              "description": "Rate limiting for tool requests"
            }
          }
        }
      }
    },
    "signature": {
      "type": "object",
      "required": ["jws"],
      "properties": {
        "jws": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "JWS Compact Serialization of the manifest (required)",
          "pattern": "^[A-Za-z0-9-_]+\\.[A-Za-z0-9-_]+\\.[A-Za-z0-9-_]+$"
        }
      }
    },
    "securityMetadata": {
      "type": "object",
      "properties": {
        "riskScore": {
          "type": "string",
          "enum": ["low", "medium", "high", "critical"],
          "description": "Optional risk assessment assigned by Security Risk Assessment Board (SRAB) - not self-assigned. To be implemented when runtime enforcement is available."
        },
        "dataClassification": {
          "type": "array",
          "items": {
            "type": "string",
            "enum": ["public", "internal", "confidential", "restricted"]
          },
          "description": "Optional data classification for monitoring & enforcement (to be implemented when runtime enforcement is available)"
        }
      }
    },
    "sandboxingRequirements": {
      "type": "object",
      "required": ["isolationLevel", "networkPolicy"],
      "properties": {
        "isolationLevel": {
          "type": "string",
          "enum": ["none", "process", "container", "vm"],
          "description": "Required isolation level for tool execution"
        },
        "allowedSystemCalls": {
          "type": "array",
          "items": {"type": "string"},
          "description": "Allowed system calls for seccomp profile"
        },
        "networkPolicy": {
          "type": "object",
          "required": ["allowedHosts"],
          "properties": {
            "allowedHosts": {
              "type": "array",
              "items": {"type": "string"},
              "description": "Allowed network destinations"
            },
            "blockedPorts": {
              "type": "array",
              "items": {"type": "integer"},
              "description": "Blocked network ports"
            }
          }
        },
        "fileSystemAccess": {
          "oneOf": [
            {
              "type": "string",
              "enum": ["none", "read-only", "read-write"],
              "description": "Simple file system access level"
            },
            {
              "type": "object",
              "properties": {
                "readOnly": {
                  "type": "array",
                  "items": {"type": "string"},
                  "description": "Read-only file paths"
                },
                "readWrite": {
                  "type": "array",
                  "items": {"type": "string"},
                  "description": "Read-write file paths"
                }
              }
            }
          ]
        }
      }
    },
    "behavioralBaseline": {
      "type": "object",
      "required": ["maxExecutionTimeMs", "expectedNetworkCalls"],
      "properties": {
        "maxExecutionTimeMs": {
          "type": "integer",
          "minimum": 1,
          "description": "Maximum expected execution time in milliseconds"
        },
        "expectedNetworkCalls": {
          "type": "array",
          "items": {"type": "string"},
          "description": "Expected network destinations for anomaly detection"
        },
        "maxResourceUsage": {
          "type": "object",
          "properties": {
            "memory": {"type": "string", "pattern": "^[0-9]+(MB|GB)$"},
            "cpu": {"type": "number", "minimum": 0, "maximum": 1}
          },
          "description": "Maximum resource usage thresholds"
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

</details>

## Tool Capability Matrix

> Implementation note: For any category that can modify state, the Host must (1) require explicit user approval, (2) attach an `Idempotency-Key` on writes, and (3) correlate the `Idempotency-Key` with the token `jti` in audit logs to detect replay.

Every tool exposed by an MCP Server must conform to one of these categories:

| Tool Category | Examples | Allowed HTTP Verbs | Side-Effect Scope | Network Scope | User Approval Required |
|---------------|----------|-------------------|-------------------|---------------|----------------------|
| **Read-Only** | iTwin Search, Asset Viewer | GET | None | Same Service API | No |
| **Client-Side UI Action** | Element Highlighter, Viewport Zoom | – | DOM mutation only | Browser-only | No |
| **Server-Mediated UI Action** | Batch Annotation, Snapshot Export | GET, POST | Client-side display only | Same Service API | No |
| **Output-Sink (Low Risk)** | Report Generator | POST | File creation in user's workspace | Cross-Service API | Yes |
| **Output-Sink (High Risk)** | Email Sender, Form Submitter | POST, PUT | External system modification | Cross-Service API | Yes |
| **Data Modification** | Form Editor, Asset Updater | PUT, PATCH, DELETE | Backend data modification | Same Service API | Yes |

### Client-Side UI Action Sandbox Implementation

**Client-Side UI Actions** are implemented as signed JavaScript modules bundled with the front-end:
- They are registered in the Discovery manifest using the pseudo-protocol `mcp://ui/{module}` and **cannot** make outbound network calls
- Browser security boundaries (Same-Origin Policy, CSP) provide the sandbox; any compromise is limited to the user's tab
- **Note:** UI-based tool capabilities require strict CSP implementation to be considered secure

## Example Manifests

<details open>
<summary><strong>Example: Building a secure search tool with read-only access</strong></summary>

```json
{
  "manifestVersion": "0.1.0",
  "spec": {
    "name": "iTwin-Search-Tool",
    "description": "Searches iTwin metadata and content with read-only access",
    "inputSchema": {
      "type": "object",
      "required": ["query"],
      "properties": {
        "query": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Search query string",
          "maxLength": 1000,
          "pattern": "^[a-zA-Z0-9\\s\\-_]+$"
        },
        "limit": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Maximum number of results",
          "minimum": 1,
          "maximum": 100,
          "default": 10
        }
      }
    }
  },
  "capability": {
    "category": "Read-Only",
    "requiresUserApproval": false
  },
  "endpoint": {
    "verb": "GET",
    "path": "/api/v2/search",
    "audience": "https://api.bentley.com"
  },
  "sandboxingDirectives": {
    "csp": {
      "sandbox": ["allow-scripts", "allow-same-origin"],
      "connectSrc": ["https://api.bentley.com"]
    }
  },
  "signature": {
    "jws": "eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiJ9.eyJtYW5pZmVzdEhhc2giOiJzaGEyNTYifQ.signature"
  },
  "sandboxingRequirements": {
    "isolationLevel": "process",
    "networkPolicy": {
      "allowedHosts": ["api.bentley.com"]
    },
    "fileSystemAccess": "read-only"
  },
  "behavioralBaseline": {
    "maxExecutionTimeMs": 5000,
    "expectedNetworkCalls": ["api.bentley.com"]
  }
}
```

</details>

<details open>
<summary><strong>Example: Creating an asset update tool with user approval</strong></summary>

```json
{
  "manifestVersion": "0.1.0", 
  "spec": {
    "name": "Asset-Update-Tool",
    "description": "Updates asset properties with validation",
    "inputSchema": {
      "type": "object",
      "required": ["assetId", "properties"],
      "properties": {
        "assetId": {
          "type": "string",
          "pattern": "^[0-9a-f-]{36}$"
        },
        "properties": {
          "type": "object",
          "additionalProperties": false
        }
      }
    }
  },
  "capability": {
    "category": "Data Modification",
    "requiresUserApproval": true
  },
  "endpoint": {
    "verb": "PUT",
    "path": "/api/v2/assets/{assetId}",
    "audience": "https://api.bentley.com"
  },
  "sandboxingDirectives": {
    "csp": {
      "sandbox": ["allow-scripts"],
      "connectSrc": ["https://api.bentley.com"]
    }
  },
  "signature": {
    "jws": "eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiJ9.eyJtYW5pZmVzdEhhc2giOiJzaGEyNTYifQ.signature"
  },
  "sandboxingRequirements": {
    "isolationLevel": "container",
    "networkPolicy": {
      "allowedHosts": ["api.bentley.com"],
      "blockedPorts": [22, 3389]
    },
    "fileSystemAccess": "none"
  },
  "behavioralBaseline": {
    "maxExecutionTimeMs": 10000,
    "expectedNetworkCalls": ["api.bentley.com"]
  }
}
```

</details>

## Security Enforcement Responsibility Matrix

<details open>
<summary><strong>How security enforcement is distributed across Discovery, Host, APIs, and Monitor</strong></summary>

The MCP security model distributes enforcement responsibilities across four distinct architectural layers. Each layer enforces what it can understand and validates, creating defense-in-depth without inappropriate coupling.

| Security Concern | Discovery Service | MCP Host | Backend APIs | Runtime Monitor |
|------------------|-------------------|-----------|--------------|----------------|
| **Server Trust & Identity** | ✅ **PRIMARY** | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ Alert on violations |
| **Manifest Schema Validation** | ✅ **PRIMARY** | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
| **Tool Capability Categories** | ✅ Basic validation | ✅ **PRIMARY** enforcement | ❌ | ✅ Monitor compliance |
| **User Authorization** | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ **PRIMARY** | ✅ Alert on failures |
| **Endpoint Context Understanding** | ❌ **Cannot do** | ❌ **Cannot do** | ✅ **ONLY** this layer can | ❌ |
| **Business Logic Validation** | ❌ **Cannot do** | ❌ **Cannot do** | ✅ **ONLY** this layer can | ❌ |
| **Parameter Semantic Validation** | ❌ Basic format only | ✅ Schema constraints | ✅ **PRIMARY** business validation | ✅ Monitor for anomalies |
| **Cross-Service Workflow Rules** | ❌ **Cannot do** | ❌ **Cannot do** | ✅ **ONLY** this layer can | ✅ Detect violations |
| **User Consent for State Changes** | ❌ | ✅ **PRIMARY** | ❌ | ✅ Monitor bypass attempts |
| **Runtime Behavioral Baselines** | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ **PRIMARY** |

</details>

<details open>
<summary><strong>Who enforces what: Mapping TCM fields to responsible components</strong></summary>

| TCM Section | Primary Consumer(s) | Enforcement Action |
|-------------|--------------------|--------------------|
| `spec` | MCP Host | Validates user arguments against enhanced parameter constraints |
| `capability.category` | MCP Host | Enforces operation boundaries per capability matrix |
| `capability.requiresUserApproval` | MCP Host + Application UI | Triggers consent dialog for state-changing operations |
| `sandboxingDirectives.csp.*` | Browser CSP Engine + MCP Client | Applies CSP sandbox restrictions to browser-based tools |
| `sandboxingDirectives.networkPolicy` | MCP Host + Runtime Monitor | Enforces server-side network access controls and rate limiting |
| `endpoint.*` | MCP Host + CI Pipeline | Host validates verb/path/audience; pipelines create network policies |
| `signature.jws` | Discovery Service + MCP Client | Verifies authenticity and integrity via JOSE signature |
| `sandboxingRequirements.isolationLevel` | MCP Server Deployer | Enforces process/container/VM isolation at deployment |
| `sandboxingRequirements.networkPolicy` | MCP Server Deployer | Configures allowed hosts, blocked ports via network policies |
| `sandboxingRequirements.fileSystemAccess` | MCP Server Deployer | Sets up file system permissions at process/container level |
| `behavioralBaseline` | Runtime Monitor | Detects execution anomalies, monitors network connections, validates isolation boundaries |

</details>

## Manifest Trust Model

The manifest trust model establishes authenticity and integrity through:

<details open>
<summary><strong>How digital signatures ensure manifest authenticity and integrity</strong></summary>

**JOSE Digital Signatures (Required):**
- All manifests must be digitally signed using JWS Compact Serialization
- RS256 or ES256 algorithms with trusted public keys
- Signatures verified against keys maintained in Discovery Service

**Trust Verification Flow:**
1. **Signing Phase**: Tool developer signs manifest with private key
2. **Registration Phase**: JWS signature embedded in manifest 
3. **Validation Phase**: Discovery Service validates signature before registration
4. **Distribution Phase**: MCP Client verifies signature using Bentley trust bundle
5. **Execution Phase**: Only verified manifests with valid trust chains execute

**Trust Failure Handling:**
- Invalid signatures reject manifest execution immediately
- Expired certificates trigger trust re-evaluation workflows
- Revoked keys invalidate all associated manifests
- Trust failures generate security audit events

</details>

## Risk Assessment Process

The optional `riskScore` field in the security metadata, when present, would be assigned through a centralized Security Risk Assessment Board (SRAB) process, not self-assigned by development teams. This field is currently optional and will be implemented when concrete runtime enforcement mechanisms are available.

<details open>
<summary><strong>Planned Security Risk Assessment Board process for tool vetting</strong></summary>

1. Tool developers submit manifests with technical documentation
2. Security architects apply objective criteria based on data sensitivity, operation impact, network scope, privilege level, and failure impact
3. Low/Medium risk tools may be auto-approved if criteria are met
4. High/Critical risk tools require SRAB review and approval
5. All decisions are documented with rationale for auditability

**Note on Data Classification:** Unlike government or military systems, Bentley's security model does not rely on data classification schemes. Security is enforced through:
- **User-Context Authorization**: All data access is governed by the authenticated user's permissions as validated by backend APIs
- **OAuth Token Scoping**: Access tokens are issued with appropriate scopes for specific services and operations
- **Service-Level Access Control**: Each backend service enforces its own access policies based on user identity and business rules

This approach aligns with Bentley's business model where data sensitivity is determined by project membership, user roles, and service-specific permissions rather than static classification labels.

</details>

## Isolation Parameter Enforcement Responsibility Matrix

This section clarifies who enforces what isolation parameters to address the ambiguity between MCP Server Deployer and MCP Host responsibilities.

### Primary Enforcement Responsibilities

| Component | Enforcement Scope | Specific Responsibilities | Deployment Context |
|-----------|-------------------|---------------------------|-------------------|
| **MCP Server Deployer** | Infrastructure & Runtime Environment | • Container/VM isolation setup<br>• Network policy configuration<br>• File system permission boundaries<br>• System call filtering (seccomp)<br>• Resource limit enforcement (CPU/memory)<br>• Process isolation boundaries | • Cloud: Container orchestration (k8s)<br>• Desktop: Process spawning & sandboxing |
| **MCP Host** | Tool Capability & Usage Governance | • Tool invocation validation against manifests<br>• Parameter constraint enforcement<br>• Capability category boundary checks<br>• User consent flow enforcement<br>• Cross-tool coordination limits<br>• Token propagation validation | • Cloud: Bentley Copilot service<br>• Desktop: Local copilot application |
| **Runtime Monitor** | Dynamic Behavioral Validation | • Real-time isolation boundary verification<br>• Behavioral baseline anomaly detection<br>• Cross-layer security event correlation<br>• Policy violation alerting & response | • Cloud: Centralized monitoring service<br>• Desktop: Local security agent |

### Defense-in-Depth Coordination

The three components work together to create layered security:

**Layer 1 (Infrastructure) - MCP Server Deployer:**
- **WHAT**: Physical/virtual boundaries and resource constraints
- **WHEN**: At deployment time and process startup
- **WHERE**: Container runtime, OS process manager, network stack
- **EXAMPLE**: Deployer creates container with read-only `/app/config`, blocks ports 22/3389, limits to 256MB RAM

**Layer 2 (Application) - MCP Host:**
- **WHAT**: Logical boundaries and usage policies  
- **WHEN**: At tool invocation time
- **WHERE**: MCP protocol layer, application business logic
- **EXAMPLE**: Host validates search query matches `^[a-zA-Z0-9\\s\\-_]+$` pattern, enforces maxLength 1000

**Layer 3 (Runtime) - Runtime Monitor:**
- **WHAT**: Dynamic validation and anomaly detection
- **WHEN**: Continuously during execution
- **WHERE**: System monitoring, network inspection, behavior analysis
- **EXAMPLE**: Monitor detects server connecting to unauthorized host, triggers isolation response

### Deployment-Specific Enforcement Examples

<details open>
<summary><strong>Real-world enforcement patterns for Kubernetes and desktop deployments</strong></summary>

**Cloud Deployment (Kubernetes):**
```yaml
<!-- original title: "MCP Server Deployer Responsibilities" removed -->
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
spec:
  securityContext:
    runAsNonRoot: true
    fsGroup: 2000
  containers:
  - name: search-server
    securityContext:
      allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
      readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
      capabilities:
        drop: ["ALL"]
    resources:
      limits:
        memory: "256Mi"
        cpu: "500m"
  - name: network-policy
    # Restrict to api.bentley.com only
```

**Desktop Deployment (Windows/macOS):**
```pseudocode
// MCP Server Deployer Responsibilities  
Process.Start(
  executable: "search-server.exe",
  sandboxProfile: {
    networkAccess: ["api.bentley.com"],
    fileSystemAccess: {
      readOnly: ["%APPDATA%/config"],
      readWrite: ["%TEMP%/search-cache"] 
    },
    resourceLimits: {
      maxMemoryMB: 256,
      maxCpuPercent: 50
    }
  }
)
```

</details>

<details open>
<summary><strong>How multiple security layers coordinate in different deployment scenarios</strong></summary>

**Pattern 1: Cloud-to-Cloud (High Security)**
- MCP Host (cloud) → MCP Server (cloud container) → Backend API (cloud)
- **Deployer**: Kubernetes with NetworkPolicies, PodSecurityStandards
- **Host**: Full manifest validation, consent flows, token delegation
- **Monitor**: Centralized logging, real-time threat detection
- **Failure Handling**: Automatic failover, circuit breakers, audit alerts

**Pattern 2: Desktop-to-Cloud**
- MCP Host (desktop) → MCP Server (local process) → Backend API (cloud)
- **Deployer**: OS process sandboxing, local firewall rules
- **Host**: Limited manifest validation, user consent prompts
- **Monitor**: Local security agent + cloud telemetry
- **Failure Handling**: Local isolation, network-level blocking, user alerts

**Pattern 3: Desktop-to-Desktop (Minimal Security)**
- MCP Host (desktop) → MCP Server (local process) → Local API (desktop)
- **Deployer**: Process isolation, file system permissions
- **Host**: Basic parameter validation, user approval dialogs
- **Monitor**: Local activity monitoring, resource usage tracking
- **Failure Handling**: Process termination, user notification, rollback

</details>

### Enforcement Failure Scenarios & Responsibilities

| Failure Scenario | Primary Responder | Secondary Responder | Action Taken |
|-------------------|-------------------|--------------------|--------------| 
| Container escape attempt | **MCP Server Deployer** | Runtime Monitor | • Container restart<br>• Enhanced isolation<br>• Security alert |
| Tool parameter injection | **MCP Host** | Runtime Monitor | • Request rejection<br>• Pattern logging<br>• User notification |
| Behavioral anomaly detected | **Runtime Monitor** | MCP Server Deployer | • Process isolation<br>• Network blocking<br>• Incident response |
| Resource limit exceeded | **MCP Server Deployer** | Runtime Monitor | • Process throttling<br>• Resource reallocation<br>• Performance alert |
| Unauthorized network access | **MCP Server Deployer** | Runtime Monitor | • Network blocking<br>• Connection termination<br>• Security investigation |
| User consent bypass attempt | **MCP Host** | Application UI | • Operation blocking<br>• Audit logging<br>• User warning |

This matrix ensures no security gaps exist between components and provides clear escalation paths for enforcement failures.
